steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Main Idea question. The passage covers three elements: (1) ceramic bowls recovered from a shipwreck, (2) a portable X-ray fluorescence analyzer that reads chemical signatures without damage, and (3) comparing those signatures to kiln-site materials to identify which kilns made the bowls. The main idea must capture all three elements together.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Because of a new technology, researchers can locate and recover more shipwrecks than they could in the past.’: The tool was used to analyze bowls already recovered from a known shipwreck, not to locate or recover shipwrecks. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Researchers have been able to identify the location of a number of Chinese kilns in operation 1,000 years ago.’: The researchers compared bowls to materials from kiln sites whose locations were already known; they identified which kilns produced the bowls, not where the kilns are. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘With the help of a special tool, researchers have determined the likely origin of bowls recovered from a shipwreck.’: This accurately captures all three elements — the tool, the bowl-origin determination, and the shipwreck context. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Before the invention of portable X-ray fluorescence, researchers needed to take a small piece out of an artifact to analyze its components.’: The passage notes only that the tool works without damaging artifacts; it never compares this to pre-invention methods or makes that prior practice the main point. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
